cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The story is called "The First Hello," and the question is about when the telephone booth was fitted. The options are A to D. I need to figure out which one is correct based on the text. First, let me read through the story again carefully. The man from the telephone department arrives a year after the phone line was installed. Then, six months later, men from public works came to put up the phone booth. Wait, so the phone line was installed a year ago, and six months after that the booth was installed. That means the phone line was installed 12 months prior, and the booth came six months after that. So the total time from the phone line being installed to the booth being put up is 18 months. But the question is about fitting a working telephone booth. The booth was installed six months after the line, so maybe the process of getting the booth took six months? But the answer options don't mention that. Let me check the options again. Option A says it was a process that had already taken 12 months. Hmm. If the phone line was installed a year ago (12 months), and the booth was put up six months later, so the booth itself was installed six months after the line. So the total time from the phone line to the booth is 1.5 years. But the question is about fitting the working booth. The story says that the phone line was installed a year ago, and six months later the booth was put up. The process of fitting the booth would be those six months? Or the entire 18 months? Wait, the first sentence says: "The man from the telephone department got off the bus... It was a year ago that the phone line had been installed, six months later men from the public works department had come to put up the phone booth." So the phone line was installed a year ago, then six months later (so 6 months after the line was installed), the booth was put up.
